title: gypsophila repens var. sabauda
intro: Gypsophila repens var. sabauda, also known as creeping baby's breath, is a low-growing, spreading perennial plant belonging to the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). Traditionally, this plant has been used for its medicinal properties, including its astringent and antiseptic properties, which have been utilized to treat various health conditions. The health benefits of Gypsophila repens var. sabauda include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant's flowers, leaves, and roots are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures that can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
